reforms.description: R&D business sector funding is increased by 0.6% of GDP, closing half of the gap to the OECD average level.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D in the business sector is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding increase has been enacted and is reflected in the national budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in R&D funding, aimed at enhancing the competitiveness of the business sector and aligning with OECD standards.
reforms.source_quote: R&D business sector funding is increased by 0.6% of GDP, closing half of the gap to the OECD average level.

reforms.description: Latvia aims to strengthen innovation and knowledge transfer by enhancing the use of EU funds to promote productivity and ensuring the continuity of effective EU-funded policy measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving knowledge transfer and productivity through effective use of EU funds can enhance innovation capabilities and economic performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to strengthen innovation and knowledge transfer has been outlined as a key policy direction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to leverage EU funds for enhancing productivity and innovation, which is crucial for Latvia's economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Using EU funds more effectively to promote productivity and ensuring the continuity of the most effective EU-funded policy measures.

reforms.description: The government plans to develop the private rental market by supporting access to long-term finance and housing with limits on rents, which is expected to improve innovation performance by addressing housing affordability issues for skilled workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to affordable housing can attract and retain skilled workers, thereby enhancing the innovation capacity of the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to develop the private rental market.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant barrier to innovation by improving housing access, which is crucial for attracting talent.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to develop the private rental market by supporting access to long-term finance and housing with limits on rents.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase spending on R&D, which is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D spending is likely to lead to higher innovation outputs and improved productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to increase spending on R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding, which is crucial for fostering innovation and addressing productivity challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The Survey recommends increased spending on R&D to support productivity and innovation.

reforms.description: The government has made considerable efforts to invest in adult learning, with participation increasing rapidly, particularly for low-skilled workers. Financial support for firms focusing on training for low-qualified workers is suggested to further enhance this initiative.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in adult learning enhances the skills of the workforce, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Participation in adult learning has increased rapidly.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses skill shortages and enhances the employability of low-skilled workers, which is significant for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government has made considerable efforts to invest in adult learning and participation has increased fast, albeit from a low level.

reforms.description: The government is working to expand work-based learning in vocational education, involving social partners in updating curricula through Sectoral Expert Councils to better align education with labor market needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding work-based learning enhances the skills of students, making them more employable and better aligned with market demands, which supports economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is working to expand work-based learning in vocational education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly addresses skill shortages and improves the alignment of education with labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The government is working to expand work-based learning, but while student numbers are increasing, they remain low.

reforms.description: The government expanded the innovation voucher scheme that finances firms’ purchase of technology extension services. Additionally, discussions have begun about the design of the Norwegian Grants programme, which will provide subsidies for the development and implementation of new innovative products and technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases access to technology and innovation for firms, enhancing their capacity to develop new products and improve productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is expected to be opened in 2020 with a total budget of EUR 14.7 million.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of the innovation voucher scheme represents a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms for firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government expanded the innovation voucher scheme that finances firms’ purchase of technology extension services.

reforms.description: Policies to improve skills and access to finance and to stimulate innovation are necessary to adapt to the specific needs of small enterprises in Latvia, which have low productivity and limited capacity to adopt new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills and access to finance will enable small enterprises to innovate and improve productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The chapter discusses policies to strengthen capital allocation and improve skills and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses critical barriers to productivity growth among small enterprises, which are vital for the overall economy.
reforms.source_quote: Policies to improve skills and access to finance and to stimulate innovation therefore always have to adapt to the specific needs of small enterprises.

reforms.description: The government plans to phase out the microenterprise tax regime, which has been found to negatively impact productivity growth, and replace it with a targeted tax credit for new firms to encourage growth and investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By removing disincentives for growth and providing support for new firms, the reform is expected to enhance investment and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government should phase out the microenterprise tax regime, considering its potentially negative impact on productivity growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant structural issue in the tax system that has widespread implications for firm growth and productivity, making it a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government should phase out the microenterprise tax regime, considering its potentially negative impact on productivity growth.

reforms.description: The government established a single development financing institution, Altum, which offers a range of start-up and growth financing through public loans, credit guarantees, and microfinance measures. In 2018, conditions for access to loans for small new businesses were relaxed to broaden the scope of clients.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances access to financing for small innovative firms, facilitating their investment and growth, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Conditions for access to loans for small new businesses have been relaxed.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of Altum and the relaxation of loan conditions represent significant steps in improving access to finance for startups, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government established a single development financing institution Altum, offering a wide range of start-up and growth financing through public loans, credit guarantees, and microfinance measures.

reforms.description: Recent policy actions have focused on developing pre-seed, seed, and venture stage capital to support small innovative firms, as previous measures were dominated by later-stage growth financing. Plans to reduce administrative burdens for venture capital funds are also underway.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to early-stage capital is essential for fostering innovation and supporting the growth of startups, which can lead to increased economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: Recent policy action focused on developing pre-seed, seed and venture stage capital.
reforms.importance_rationale: The focus on early-stage capital development is a significant shift that addresses a critical gap in the financing landscape for startups, promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Recent policy action focused on developing pre-seed, seed and venture stage capital, as previously available measures were dominated by later-stage growth financing.

reforms.description: The Latvian government eased the labour market tests for hiring foreign skilled workers in 237 professions facing severe skills shortages, aiming to attract talent and address skill mismatches in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled workers can enhance the talent pool, fostering innovation and productivity growth in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has eased the labour market tests for hiring foreign skilled workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical barrier to firm growth and innovation by improving access to skilled labour, which is essential for productivity.
reforms.source_quote: In 2018, the government eased the labour market tests for the hiring of foreign skilled workers in 237 professions where severe skills shortages are foreseen.

reforms.description: The government has improved vocational education and training (VET) by consolidating VET schools into Vocational Education Competence Centres (VECCs), which provide pedagogical assistance and disseminate updated training programmes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing VET aligns education with labour market needs, improving the skills of the workforce and supporting innovation in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has improved the implementation of VET by consolidating VET schools and upgrading them into VECCs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the quality and relevance of vocational training, which is crucial for addressing skill shortages and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has also improved the implementation of VET by consolidating VET schools and gradually upgrading VET schools with more than 500 students into Vocational Education Competence Centres.

reforms.description: The government has required higher education institutions to coordinate their development strategies and infrastructure investment plans with industry representatives since 2017, aiming to enhance the relevance of academic research to market needs.
reforms.package_name: Higher Education and Industry Coordination Initiative
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning academic research with industry needs, this reform is expected to foster innovation and improve productivity in the long term.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has required coordination since 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in governance that aims to enhance the responsiveness of higher education to economic needs, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Since 2017, the government has required higher education institutions to coordinate their development strategies and infrastructure investment plans with industry representatives.

reforms.description: The government has expanded adult education programmes since 2017 using European Social Fund financing, targeting skill shortages in the labor market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the skills of the adult population, this reform aims to improve workforce productivity and adaptability to changing economic conditions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has expanded adult education programmes since 2017.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the adult education landscape, addressing skill shortages and improving workforce capabilities, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has expanded adult education programmes since 2017 using European Social Fund financing.

reforms.description: The government is implementing the Innovation Motivation Program, which organizes networking events and promotes innovative entrepreneurship, particularly among youth, to increase SME engagement in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship among youth, the program aims to enhance the future pool of innovators and entrepreneurs, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The program has been actively organizing networking events and promoting entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The program represents a significant effort to engage youth in innovation, which is crucial for building a sustainable startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government has been implementing the Innovation Motivation Program, which organizes numerous networking events and promotion of innovative entrepreneurship especially among the youth.

reforms.description: The government aims to strengthen innovation and knowledge transfer by promoting the sharing of resources among universities and research institutions, improving working conditions for researchers, and consolidating innovation support into a single agency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry is expected to lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to strengthen innovation and knowledge transfer has been outlined in policy recommendations.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the innovation ecosystem in Latvia by consolidating support mechanisms and improving collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Strengthening innovation and knowledge transfer by promoting sharing of resources of universities and research institutions.
